meta.setting: Hard-rock mine mouth above French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in mining labor and underground risk

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek mining history and regional work conditions.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce and material culture.

image_prompt.subject: hard-rock miners at a timbered tunnel mouth, one holding a candle box while another sets drill steel beside a powder keg and ore cart rails
image_prompt.scene: gray dawn outside a mine adit; damp timber, mule tracks, tailings, wet coats, one man coughing into a sleeve, and pine hillside scarred by work
image_prompt.composition: square group scene with the black tunnel mouth as the heavy center, miners arranged by task instead of pose, and the ore cart rail pulling the viewer into darkness
image_prompt.camera: waist-height frontal angle with slight low-poly stiffness in tools and hands, like a grounded in-engine mission plate
material_cues: drill steel rubbed bright at the grip
powder keg wood and iron hoops
soot on faces and shirt collars
ore cart iron and dull rails
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: The tunnel should look like a job, not an adventure: dark, cold, and owned by somebody absent. Let the men look competent, tired, and already counting risks.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine labor scene
render_traits: slightly simplified miner silhouettes and tool geometry
modest texture resolution on timber, stone, cloth, and soot
hard ambient occlusion inside tunnel mouth and under cart rails
baked gray dawn light with small warm lamp accents
believable 1899 mining clothing, tools, and powder equipment
style_modifiers.palette: quartz gray, wet timber brown, soot black, faded denim, dull iron, candle amber, mule hide brown, and pine green.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable labels, notices, or crate markings. Any paper or keg marks must be indistinct.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The miners, tunnel mouth, drill steel, powder keg, and ore rails are all fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to 1899 mining labor.
The image shows work danger and company pressure without modern equipment.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
